ne: ∃ s : X, S s
isaprop (∑ s : X, S s ∧ (∀ t : X, S t ⇒ s ≤ t))%type
 
X: WellOrderedSet
S: hsubtype X
ne: ∃ s : X, S s
isTotalOrder (WOrel X)
 apply WO_isTotalOrder.
  
X: WellOrderedSet
S: hsubtype X
ne: ∃ s : X, S s
(∑ s : X, S s ∧ (∀ t : X, S t ⇒ s ≤ t))%type
 exact (WO_theSmallest S ne).
Defined.

(* Close these for now *)
Local Close Scope set.
Local Close Scope prop.

(** Functions of well-ordered sets that preserve the ordering and initial segments *)
Definition iswofun {X Y : WellOrderedSet} (f : X → Y) : UU :=
  (iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f) ×
  (∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ (z : X), z < x × f z = y).


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
isaprop (iswofun f)


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
isaprop (iswofun f)


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h: iswofun f
t, t0: X
t1: t ≤ t0
isaprop (f t ≤ f t0)
X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h: iswofun f
t: X
t0: Y
t1: t0 < f t
isaprop (∃ z : X, z < t × f z = t0)


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h: iswofun f
t, t0: X
t1: t ≤ t0
isaprop (f t ≤ f t0)
 now apply propproperty.

X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h: iswofun f
t: X
t0: Y
t1: t0 < f t
isaprop (∃ z : X, z < t × f z = t0)
 now apply isapropishinh.
Qed.

Definition wofun (X Y : WellOrderedSet) : UU := ∑ (f : X -> Y), iswofun f.

Definition pr1wofun (X Y : WellOrderedSet) : wofun X Y → (X → Y) := @pr1 _ _.


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f, g: wofun X Y
pr1 f = pr1 g → f = g


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f, g: wofun X Y
pr1 f = pr1 g → f = g


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f, g: wofun X Y
Heq: (pr1 f = pr1 g)%type
(f = g)%type


X, Y: WellOrderedSet
f, g: wofun X Y
Heq: (pr1 f = pr1 g)%type
isPredicate iswofun

now intros h; apply isaprop_iswofun.
Qed.


X: WellOrderedSet
iswofun (idfun X)


X: WellOrderedSet
iswofun (idfun X)


X: WellOrderedSet
iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel X) (idfun X)
X: WellOrderedSet
∏ x y : X,
y < idfun X x → ∃ z : X, z < x × idfun X z = y


X: WellOrderedSet
iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel X) (idfun X)
 now intros x.

X: WellOrderedSet
∏ x y : X,
y < idfun X x → ∃ z : X, z < x × idfun X z = y
 
X: WellOrderedSet
x, y: X
hxy: y < idfun X x
∃ z : X, z < x × idfun X z = y

  now apply hinhpr; exists y.
Qed.


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: wofun X Y
g: wofun Y Z
iswofun (pr1 g ∘ pr1 f)


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: wofun X Y
g: wofun Y Z
iswofun (pr1 g ∘ pr1 f)


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: wofun Y Z
iswofun (pr1 g ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f))


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
iswofun (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f))


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Z)
  (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f))
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
∏ (x : X) (y : Z),
y < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
→ ∃ z : X,
  z < x
  × (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) z = y


X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Z)
  (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f))
 
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
x, y: X
hxy: x ≤ y
(pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
≤ (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) y

  exact (h1g _ _ (h1f _ _ hxy)).

X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
∏ (x : X) (y : Z),
y < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
→ ∃ z : X,
  z < x
  × (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) z = y
 
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
x: X
z: Z
hf: z < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
∃ z0 : X,
z0 < x
× (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) z0 = z

  
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
x: X
z: Z
hf: z < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
y: Y
h1y: y < f x
h2y: g y = z
∃ z0 : X,
z0 < x
× (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) z0 = z

  
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
x: X
z: Z
hf: z < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
y: Y
h1y: y < f x
h2y: g y = z
x': X
h1x': x' < x
h2x': f x' = y
∃ z0 : X,
z0 < x
× (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) z0 = z

  
X, Y, Z: WellOrderedSet
f: X → Y
h1f: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel X) (WOrel Y) f
h2f: ∏ (x : X) (y : Y), y < f x → ∃ z : X, z < x × f z = y
g: Y → Z
h1g: iscomprelrelfun (WOrel Y) (WOrel Z) g
h2g: ∏ (x : Y) (y : Z), y < g x → ∃ z : Y, z < x × g z = y
x: X
z: Z
hf: z < (pr1 (g,, h1g,, h2g) ∘ pr1 (f,, h1f,, h2f)) x
y: Y
h1y: y < f x
h2y: g y = z
x': X
h1x': x' < x
h2x': f x' = y
neg (x ≤ x') × (g (f x') = z)%type

  now rewrite h2x'.
Qed.

Local Open Scope set.
Local Open Scope prop.

(** The empty set is well-ordered *)

WellOrderedSet


WellOrderedSet


WellOrdering (∅,, isasetempty)


hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty)
(λ x : hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty),
 (λ R : hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty),
  hProp_to_hSet (isWellOrder R)) x) ?pr1


hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty)
 intros [].

(λ x : hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty),
 (λ R : hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty),
  hProp_to_hSet (isWellOrder R)) x)
  ((λ X : ∅,, isasetempty,
    match X return (∅,, isasetempty → hProp) with
    end)
   :
   hrel_set (∅,, isasetempty))
 abstract (repeat split; try (now intros []);
            now intros T t'; apply (squash_to_hProp t'); intros [[]]).
Defined.

(** The unit set is well-ordered *)

WellOrderedSet


WellOrderedSet


WellOrdering (unit,, isasetunit)


hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit)
(λ x : hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit),
 (λ R : hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit),
  hProp_to_hSet (isWellOrder R)) x) ?pr1


hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit)
 
x, y: unit,, isasetunit
hProp

  
x, y: unit,, isasetunit
isaprop (x = y)

  abstract (apply isapropifcontr, isapropunit).

(λ x : hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit),
 (λ R : hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit),
  hProp_to_hSet (isWellOrder R)) x)
  ((λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
    (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
   :
   hrel_set (unit,, isasetunit))
 
istrans
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
isantisymm
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
istotal
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
hasSmallest
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)

  
istrans
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
 now intros x y z [].
  
isantisymm
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
 now intros x.
  
istotal
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
 
H: hProp
H2: ((tt = tt),, unit_woset_subproof tt tt)
⨿ ((tt = tt),, unit_woset_subproof tt tt) → H
H

    now apply H2, inl.
  
hasSmallest
  (λ x y : unit,, isasetunit,
   (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)
 
T: hsubtype (unit,, isasetunit)
H: T tt
∃ x : unit,, isasetunit,
T x
∧ (∀ y : unit,, isasetunit,
   T y ⇒ (x = y),, unit_woset_subproof x y)

    
T: hsubtype (unit,, isasetunit)
H: T tt
T tt
∧ (∀ y : unit,, isasetunit,
   T y ⇒ (tt = y),, unit_woset_subproof tt y)

    now split; [|intros []].
Defined.



isaset WellOrderedSet


isaset WellOrderedSet

  (*
     First show that an order preserving equivalence f : X ≃ Y of well
     ordered sets has the property that for any x, f x is least element
     greater than f y for every y < x.  Now given also an order
     preserving f' : X ≃ Y, take the smallest x where f x ≠ f y.  Since
     they agree on previous values, they agree at x, too, using the previous
     statement.
   *)

Abort.

(* ** Transfinite recursion *)

(*
   We should be able to prove Zorn's lemma from transfinite recursion, or,
   better yet, make it unneeded.
 *)


X: WellOrderedSet
P: X → Type
LEM
→ (∏ x : X, (∏ y : X, y < x → P y) → P x)
  → ∏ x : X, P x


X: WellOrderedSet
P: X → Type
LEM
→ (∏ x : X, (∏ y : X, y < x → P y) → P x)
  → ∏ x : X, P x

  
X: WellOrderedSet
P: X → Type
g: LEM
(∏ x : X, (∏ y : X, y < x → P y) → P x) → ∏ x : X, P x

  (*
     Consider subsets C of X that are initial segments for the ordering,
     each of which is equipped with a section f of P and a proof of
     ∏ x ∈ C, g x = f x (λ y, g y).  One may say that f is guided by g.
     Then two pairs (C,f), (C',f') agree on their common intersection, which
     is C or C', and thus the union U of all their graphs is a maximal guided
     function.  If U were a proper subset, then its upper bound
     could be added to U, contradiction, so U = X.
   *)

Abort.


X: Type
(∑ Y : hSet, ∏ f : Y → X, ¬ isincl f)%type


X: Type
(∑ Y : hSet, ∏ f : Y → X, ¬ isincl f)%type

  (*
     This lemma is useful in arguments by contradiction, where one uses
     transfinite recursion to define an injective function f, after first
     equipping Y with a well ordering.

     To prove it, let Y be the set of subsets of π_0 (X).
   *)


Abort.
